---
title: "Bin selection"
author: "Dhevin Fernando"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Select bins based on checkm results
channge directory to checkm folder

```bash
cd checkm
```
view the checkm_results_bbd.txt file

```bash
head checkm_results_bbd.txt 
```
show more lines with

```bash
head -n 20 checkm_results_bbd.txt 
```
I am now going to extract the bins with a completeness greater than 15%

this command will printn the lines where the 13th column has a value greater than 15

```bash
cat checkm_results_bbd.txt | awk '$13>15'
```
lets extract the bin IDs for this selection with the following command

```bash
cat checkm_results_bbd.txt | awk '$13>15{print $1}'
```
this extraction will then be output to a file

```bash
cat checkm_results_bbd.txt | awk '$13>15{print $1}' > bbd_topbins.txt
```
